Ukraine’s New Refinery Tactic Is Fueling Russia’s Gasoline Crisis
headlines.summary: Russia’s deputy prime minister kept walking but couldn't shake off a gaggle of reporters with questions about the country’s growing fuel crisis. Aleksandr Novak admitted oil production had fallen due to “unscheduled maintenance” at refineries but avoided saying why it was needed.

headlines.title: Russia Eases Environmental Rules for Domestic Fuel amid Shortages
headlines.summary: Russia’s government has authorized some refineries to produce gasoline and diesel with higher sulfur content and other lower environmental specifications in a bid to alleviate the ongoing fuel shortages, Russian daily Kommersant reported on Monday. headlines.summary: Without ever discovering another barrel, at today’s prices, there is more than $42 trillion in crude oil trapped in existing oilfields, awaiting the deployment of advanced new technology that makes the impossible possible. headlines.title: Britain Intercepts Russian Oil Tanker Linked to Shadow Fleet
headlines.summary: British armed forces have intercepted a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in the English Channel during the early hours of Sunday morning, the prime minister has said. headlines.title: First LNG Tanker Clears Hormuz After U.S.-Iran Deal Announcement
headlines.summary: An LNG carrier successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz early on Monday, the first tanker carrying energy products to clear the chokepoint since the U.S. and Iran announced a deal to reopen the Strait later this week. headlines.title: China’s Return to the Oil Market Could Boost Inflation
headlines.summary: The U.S.-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz could prompt China to return to buying more crude after months of multi-year-low purchases, which could reignite inflationary pressures despite the expected ease of oil flows from the Middle East. headlines.title: U.S.-Iran Deal Doesn’t Mean a Swift Return of Oil and Gas Flows
headlines.summary: The U.S.-Iran deal and the potentially imminent reopening of the Strait of Hormuz do not mean that oil and gas trade will quickly return to its previous levels. headlines.title: Australian Regulator Clears LNG Strike
headlines.summary: The Australian Fair Work Commission has denied Inpex a request to stop a strike at the Ichthys facility that would affect production and exports at the 9.2-million-ton facility. headlines.title: LNG Tanker Heads for Hormuz Amid News of Reopening
headlines.summary: A liquefied natural gas tanker has headed for the Strait of Hormuz, following an announcement by President Trump that he had struck a deal with Iran for the reopening of the chokepoint. Itan confirmed the preliminary agreement. The agreement is scheduled to be signed on Friday, w

headlines.title: Oil Prices Plunge as U.S. and Iran Reach Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Oil prices plunged in early Monday trading in Asia after the U.S. and Iran announced a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz more than 100 days after its closure. At the time of writing, Brent crude had dropped 3.95% to trade at $83.88 per barrel, while WTI had fallen 4.62% to $80.
